channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire? — 65 replies — 260.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire?

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-06-03 05:18 UTC

OP

What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire? Assuming one lives in USA

Early replies (first 3)

62299698
>>62299690
Just do nothing and wait, we will all be billionaires within the next decade

62299702
>Assuming one lives in USA
the first step is to get the fuck out of that dangerous brown shithole and go to Europe

62299711
>>62299690
Inheritance

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302274
>>62299702
I'd rather not get stabbed by browns

62302488
>>62299690
Inherit a billion dollars and blow 99% of it partying.

62302523
>>62299690
Save about $2800 a month and invest it in something safe, like an index fund. At an average return of 7%, you'll have 2mil in 20 years.

Though, inflation will eat a lot of that up, at least 40%, and your 2mil will spend like 1.2mil. Invest about $4k a month instead to get ahead of it. Its the only way surefire way.

62302537
>>62302523
most people in the US don't make $4k per month after taxes, let alone have that much left over after expenses.

62304411
>>62302523
>invest 1 million a month in index funds, after only one month you’ll have 1 million (enough to retire)

62304496
>>62302537
Get a $96k per year remote job, and slum it in the deep south for 20 years. You might be able to get away living off only $48k a year. Or find the money some other way, I'm just telling you whats required.
>>62304411
You're forgetting about the magic of compound interest friend. $4k a month is only $960k after 20 years, coumpound interet is giving you a free million dollars.

62304513
buy link and delude yourself into believing $1k eoy. there are lots of (essentially) millionaires like this on this board

62305112
>>62299690
>multimillionaire
>2026
Was born in a rich family.
None others.

62305121
>>62305112
Not meaning to move the goal post, but this is assuming one does not have outside help starting from $0

62305139
>>62302523
>>62299716
> low-cost index funds
Only worked in boomer casino due to FED bank inflation that inflated all assets,
including stocks.
Won't work in the next 20 years.
Be worried that the casino would drop 30-50%, and you'd lose this much and won't recover.

62305158
>>62305121
Buddy.
Let's assume you have $1000.
You need to go 1000x for it to be $1 mil.
10000x to have 10 mil.
The odds are as low as winning a lottery.
I'd start at getting a job where you can earn enough so that you can have enough disposable income to invest.

62305193
>>62305158
Already got a job and saved up 160k in a few years living frugally. I can confirm this is the most sure way to 6 figs, but going from 160k to 2m+ is going to take a lifetime at my current rate. Certainly not low stress either.

62306348
>>62305139
Nothing is ever certain, but the stock market has an average return of 10% long term, everyone and their grandmother keeps their money in it. There would literally be mass suicides if it dropped 30%, so its in the best interest of the fed and every other investor to keep it growing. Conspiracy theories of its demise have existed for decades, and its still here.

62306361
>>62305193
Yeah 20 years if you dont add any more money, but youre obviously not going to do that. Just keep saving and investing and making good choices and you'll be financially independent in probaby 10 years.

62306493
>>62304411
what's the catch?

62306609
>>62306348
>There would literally be mass suicides if it dropped 30%
you mean the gfc?

62306632
>>62299690
picking a super bowl winner, not turning on the game and just playing vidya and then wake up the next morning forgetting you even placed the bet.

62306693
>>62299690
depends on iq
for me its algorithmic trading with rebalancing mag 7 and kabsa

62306705
>>62299690
0dtes

62307416
>>62300255
Dumb fucking pajeet nigger monkey.

62307546
>>62299690
get a high paying job at a hedge fund then index meme i thought this was obvious

62307548
>>62307416
lmaooo true

62307616
Not stress-free but it involves buying a fat stack of toad.

62307657
>>62299690
Depends how old you are
But for people under 30, just have a high savings rate (spend as little as possible on cars and eating/drinking as a form of entertainment, they're probably the biggest budget killers for most people), take advantage of every tax advantaged account (401k, hsa, etc.) available to you, and you'll probably hit millionaire status in 15-30 years, depending on what your savings rate was, which is of course heavily determined by income. But any midwit making 75-100k could do this.

62307680
>>62299690
you can actually go on google and ask how most millionaires made their money by percentage.

protip: Nobody on /biz/ knows what they're talking about.

second protip: Because nobody here knows how other people get rich, you can safely bet nobody here is actually rich.

62307719
>>62307680
Old news. Most millionaires today bought a decent house ten years ago.
A $250k house in 2016 is now worth over a million almost everywhere in the US.

It's literally that simple. A million just ain't what it used to be (because of inflation and the federal reserve scam).

62307733
>>62307719
notice that owning a house does not appear in the list

your headcanon is something I started because I made half a million buying a house.

but the other half is my business.

the average american has a $400k house and about $1000 cash, making them NOT a millionaire. Also paid off houses are pretty fucking rare.

62307829
>>62307733
>notice that owning a house does not appear in the list
Yeah my point was explicitly that the list is wrong and most people are paper millionaires because of housing equity and not having a reason to move in the last decade. Not because they all go around starting businesses and becoming corporate executives.

It's like how those lists of the richest people in the world always have a little asterisk: they explicitly exclude royalty and nobility. Which is 80% of the world's wealthiest people.

62307847
>>62307829
>Yeah my point was explicitly that the list is wrong
weird how you're right and everyone that studies wealth is wrong.

you should find a way to cash in on your superior ass-knowledge

62307850
>>62299690
Buy chain*nk <$9

item.title: >not even any pink wojaks as BTC collapses It's well and truly fucking over — 25 replies — 92.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
>not even any pink wojaks as BTC collapses It's well and truly fucking over

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-06-03 05:02 UTC

OP

>not even any pink wojaks as BTC collapses
It's well and truly fucking over

Early replies (first 3)

62304356
feels like everyone has given up on crypto. i genuinely miss the faggots who tattood safemoon icon on their arms. without those faggots there is no bigger fool to buy our bags

62304364
>>62304356
Did it feel like this in other crypto winters?

I remember just losing interest for years before.

62304383
>>62304364
2018 felt exactly like this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62304727
>>62304364
its never gone down this much

62304736
>>62304727
Sure about that? I think it's gone down more as a percentage before.

62304745
>>62304736
idk if we count from the top its like 50% down. has that really happened in the past

62304756
>>62304745
It usually goes down over 70%

62304767
>>62304383
meh, I was here in December 2018 and this honestly feels worse. There is no hope now that Trump, CZ and Saylor rugged most goyim investors. If you factor in diminishing returns with EVERY bullrun the average normie doesn't care anymore about crypto when AI stocks pump like altcoins in 2017

62304773
>>62304767
Yes yes it's so over because [INSERT CURRENT DOOM AND GLOOM NARRATIVES]

62304780
>>62304756
if that happens now saylor is sinking the ship with no survivors

62304788
>>62304773
I'm not saying it's over you retarded fag, I'm just saying the sentiment is horrible for OBVIOUS reasons. Learn to read the room nigga, most normies hate crypto and have been all-in stocks since 2022

62304791
>>62304788
>normies

62304799
>>62304791
>duuuude I don't care about normies
good luck selling your bags then

62304828
>>62304799
>he doesn't even understand

62304840
>>62304828
>da gubmint and banks will pump my bags
yeah I've been hearing this bullshit since 2017, wake the fuck up

62304856
>>62304840
just go back ..

62304867
>>62304364
There's a limit to the amount of dumb money in the world that's available for the crypto ponzi. Sooner or later the price will hit a wall.

62307476
>>62304161
I just bought 10 links

62307486
I am buying Hyperliquid or Solana in November, if Bitcoin drop to 30k, it will only 4x when it return to its ATH. I want to 10x at least my capital in 3 years.

62307507
>>62307486
yeh those are in my top 3 buy's if Bitcoin hits 30k, I was literally thinking this exact scenario like an hour ago

62307788
>>62304867
The wall is here.
Everyone knows that putting money into crypto is a retarded move now. Only a few gamblers are trying to cheat each other. And some retards who cannot give up on the sunk cost.

62307802
>>62304161
board traffic is way down since changes to capcha and email registration apparently decimated bots and shills.

62307805
>>62304161
Don't insult my religion, Get a life.

62307810
>>62304745
>50% down has never happened
It's happened during every single bullrun lol. Yes, bull run.

62307816
>>62307486
Its facing network death as miners shift their datacentres into AI, it's an obsolete DLT and miners are dumping their bitcoin hoardes, havening is making future mining worthless and energy costs make it unprofitable, interest rates are not being cut and then here is (you)

Everything is born and with that everything dies. You think things stay the same forever. Bitcoin rose during a time when borrowed money was essentially free. That's over and it's also over for bitcoins network unless you get on a hamster wheel and start mining it because no one else will.

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 62 replies — 33.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-06-03 03:26 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62302809
>>62302807
Saylor cuckold fanboys are too retarded to understand this. Imagine worshipping a boomer faggot from the dotcom bubble who spent 6 years buying "dips" and is now down -5 billion dollars kek

62302811
>>62302809
the sentiment is obvious outside of delusional saylormaxis, maybe not soon but eventually this house of cards topples

62302815
>>62302811
the scary part is how plenty of BTC maxis fell for his bullshit. MSTR is a giant ponzi

62303046
>SELL ORGANS KEEP BITCOIN NEVER SELL BITCOIN
>sells insanely small fraction of bitcoin
>see it's literally nothing, a drop in the ocean
>also he's just selling to prove he can for the bankers because such a small fraction provides confidence it's all truly worth that
retarded

62303090
>>62302809
>full stops instead of commas
you third worlders ruined this board

62303101
>>62299379
Not an mstr holder but getting their debt rated is probably going to open up the capital markets to them.

62303404
>>62299564
Is my 12btc in danger? I keep holding

62303647
>>62299187
he's just refinancing, sell 400k btc at 65k, rebuy at 20k, sell bitcoin to buy bitcoin, diamond hands

62303655
>>62299202
to buy more bitcoin at cheaper price, when yoi're holding so much btc market manipulation = refinancting

62303658
>>62299187
Have you ever heard of a death spiral? The lower it goes, the more he needs to sell.

Kek - baggies.

62303726
>>62299202
it was a test send. i always send a small amount first to see if i got the right wallet, etc.

62304527
>>62303404
>>62302272
If you held through March 2020, Dec 2022, Feb 2026 then I'm sure you will continue to hold
BUT you are literally missing out on the biggest bullrun in stock market history
You are literally sidelined right now and its clear people are rotating out of BTC and into stock (AI boom) even if you think its a bubble

62304534
>>62303090
>t. shitskin amerimutt
we use it like this in Europe

62304789
>>62299202
32 is 2 to the power of 5. 2+5 is 7. 7 is his lucky number.

62304804
this is going to be so fucking epic once saylors goes bankrupt. btc will go back to 3k. i might buy then

62304814
>>62299202
>>62304534
32 is 23 backwards

62305021
>>62299187
Nobody wants "Bitcoiners to get shaken out". That's like asking a casino to kick out all their players.

62305074
>>62305021
saylor is the casino, holding 15% of all bitcoin, if he sells you sell, if he buys you buy, try betting against the house

62305458
>>62303404
Hold and cry

62305981
>>62303658
My btc is safe

62306089
>>62299187

62306846
>>62301590
didn't he invest in BTC because it was the most perfect form of currency in the first place? Why would he need to prove BTC is liquid? Everybody knows it's liquid. You don't need MSTR selling at a loss to prove that.
This is the most retarded chutzpah i've ever heard. It just fucking reeks of fear, these niggerjews are so stupid they don't realize they pour gasoline on a fire they themselves started.

62306852
>>62302729
>comments are nearly 100% about "this ship is sinking, Saylor is broke, ponzi collapsing"
I mean, that's currently how much he's under. That was the most predictable fiasco on earth, everybody called it, and now it's happening, nobody is surprised.

62306876
>>62299187
The fact that he sold any is cause for concern.

62306905
>>62306852
And yet he isn't going to sell any meaningful quantity, no matter how much FUD you post

62306930
>>62306905
Wanna bet on this?
Will you bake a bread and publicly apologize on /biz/ for being wrong if he sells any meaningful amount (let settle this at 1000 BTC, ok?) before EOY?
I will screencap and apologize on 12/31 if he solded less than 1K btc total during the year. Deal?

62306967
>>62306930
Sure. Pinky promise.

62307478
>>62303404
Even if some major players sell, BTC isn't going to zero. It can drop, sure, but that's not the same thing. In the meantime, you can stake your BTC through self-custodial options and earn while holding long term.

62307489
>>62299553
Don't worry about that

62307496
>>62302691
They should have done market buys

OTC buying is just bullshit

item.title: ICP flipped ASTER — 16 replies — 25.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
ICP flipped ASTER

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-03 04:40 UTC

OP

Let’s fucking go

Early replies (first 3)

62304868
cool now post chart

62304877
>>62304868
Who cares about the chart. Focus on the team and what they’re building.

62304881
>>62304868
the biggest bart in crypto history

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62304883
>>62304868
This chart?

62304884
>>62304877
nothing then

62304886
>>62304884
Caffeine usage is up 30% since v3. So yea more people are building on ICP

62304903
>>62304886
30% of zero equals zero.

62304908
Nice! I'm only down 82% now

62304932
>>62304861
>Some shit flipped some shit
Ok

62304959
CZ will keep nuking crypto until you all stop using Hyperliquid and start using Asster. Keep making him angry at your own risk.

62304993
>>62304861
>ICP now cheaper than when I bought my first assets
wait a minute
>ICP now as cheap as October 2023
wait a second

Perhaps it's time I finally buy some. When I first started looking around on /biz/ I concluded ICP was a bit too high for a newbie like me + saw potential but more long-term.
Figured I should make gains on lower-cap, newer projects to rotate into it (/biz/ helped me snag multiple 10x and a 30x). Now just might be the time. I will DYOR.

62305104
>>62304959
poor lil pingpong :(

62307490
>>62304993
just buy 1000 and hodl

62307549
>>62304861
What the fuck even is Aster?

62307704
>>62304877
"I'm in it for the tech"

62307755
>>62304959
>>62305104
>mysterious hack on Lighter last year caused it to dump and never recover
>got Jackdex delisted and basically blacklisted
>Chinese insiders just ruined EdgeX, halving it
>CZ is now nuking BTC to kill Hype, hype is still pumping
Unironically what is his problem
Why can't he just let other perp dexes -exist-

>>62307549
chinkzilla created Aster to try and corner the perpdex market the way bnb was trying to kill eth and he is trying to chinkishly kill all other perpdexes. But Hype has first mover advantage like Eth.

item.title: Day 1733 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 1733 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-03 05:05 UTC

OP

Day 1733 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Early replies (first 3)

62304984
rope yourself you fraudulent spamming fuck

62306211
>>62304984
>>62304726
live forever and prosper you inglorious bastard adorable snale

62306233
Lest we forget snail, lest we forget

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62306381
>>62304726
Slow and steady wins the race! All we need to do is finish getting all of the world's homosexuals to buy btc, and we'll go to the moon!

62306799
>>62304726
I still think about you sometimes. Looking forward to being back in later this year.

62307824
>>62306211
Regarding yesterday, I'm doing good. Finished doing windows recently. Installing a stove next.

item.title: I TOLD YOU SO MUAHAHAHA >>62288629 — 28 replies — 11.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I TOLD YOU SO MUAHAHAHA >>62288629

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-06-03 02:53 UTC

OP

I TOLD YOU SO MUAHAHAHA

>>62288629

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/michael-saylors-bitcoin-treasury-firm-122216992.html

Early replies (first 3)

62298232
Bloody bastard! He made promise he wouldnt sell god fucking damnit

62298234
Anyone gets the feeling that Saylor won’t be part of the next bull run?

62298235
It's not enough, Saylor needs to be totally cleaned out for crypto to ever rise again.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298248
I hope this retarded permabull learns from this lesson

62298253
buy high, sell low

the biz way

62298267
BTC sub 60k today

Chainlink suppression killed it

62298280
>>62298216
Kek what a retard

62298598
32 btc sold, literal nothing burger.

62298622
>>62298598
32 today, it's only the beginning, once we are sub 60k he will panic sell thousands. Screencap this.

62298715
Pretty bearish for the asset backed ponzi scheme it's having to liquidate asset instead of just diluting more.

62298807
This was announced one month ago during the earnings call:

"We'll probably sell some Bitcoin to fund a dividend just to inoculate the market—just to send the message that we did it,” he said during the Q&A.

100% of the posters in this thread so far didn't acknowledge this, which proves his point that the headline will cause a lot of anti-BTC celebrations, so it is better to get the headline out of the way when the time is right.

Company officials already stated many months ago that they might sell BTC if it is considered helpful. For example, if the company's common stock is severely undervalued, trading some of its BTC for stock is probably a good deal.

The idea that the company cannot ever possibly sell any BTC was always absurd (Saylor's early statements on this probably didn't help), and they're right to start breaking this imagined taboo because people (like the posters here) only read the headlines.

62301366
>>62298807
>Saylor's early statements on this probably didn't help
Correct. Too many this is the only thing that matters.

62301536
>>62298232
he's selling to fund STRC dividends retard. this is what keeps the flywheel going

62302510
>>62302507
if only you knew how bad things really are

62302513
>>62301536
strc scam is collapsing, he barely has enough to pay dividends for a couple months

62302528
>>62298807
they are so broke that they needed 32 BTC?

62302544
>>62302507
Did he really post this? If so, that's an interesting message.

62302756
He sold a little bit now so in the future when he sells again he can say, look, it’s no big deal, I’ve sold before remember?

62302777
>>62302507
>last name is saylor
>he's getting off the boat
holy pottery

62302806
>>62298216
LUNA 2.0 inc

the moron is paying 11.5% on money he dont got

62302821
>>62302777
checked

IT'S OVER

62302859
>>62302544
Yeah. I interpreted it as Saylor is fleeing Bitcoin the sinking ship on his personal life raft if his investor’s money

62304470
either this shit is about to crater, or he only did it because there was money to be made on polymarket betting he did. either way, the real bull run doesnt begin until saylor gets liquidated

62304478
Can someone tell me where he is getting money to service his debt?

62304831
>want to buy cheapies
>sell insignificant amount
>market spazzes out
>buy cheapies

62307409
>>62304831
moron

item.title: if LINK went to $30 I would be a millionaire. — 17 replies — 8.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
if LINK went to $30 I would be a millionaire.

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-06-03 03:19 UTC

OP

if LINK went to $30 I would be a millionaire. It's never going to happen.

Early replies (first 3)

62297954
>>62297950
It already happened and you didn't sell, now suffer.

62298029
>>62297950
they shill link on this board to attract exit liquidity. Stop buying retarded shit coin like link that is just an elite piggy bank.

62298095
imagine not selling that dead pisscoin and going all in on hype, you're guaranteed to be a millionaire in 2-3 months tops

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62298150
>>62297950
No, it won't, it will top up 15$ next bull. Let the stinkies deal in the real world

62298197
>>62297950
correct, stinkie
correct

62299493
>>62297950
Adem fvcking won bros....

62299870
>>62297950
>he’s not already a millionaire at $9 LINK
ngmi

62300431
>>62299493
>won

62300822
>>62297950
if link went to $300 i'd be a millionaire. good thing its going to $1000.

62300905
insane how many stinkers are still around

62302760
>>62300431
>>62300822
Adem WON, cuckolds LOST

TNN
THE cuckolds of crypto
price + chart

62302779
>>62297950
then plan accordingly

62302860
It's been so long that nulinkers don't even attempt to roleplay as sub-20 cent buyers anymore.

62303949
>>62302779
cuckolds don't plan, they just HODL like a bunch of cucks while Sirgay dumps on them.

62303961
>>62297950
huh it was $30 a few months ago why didnt you sell then? It was $50 a few years ago when I bought

62304248
>>62302860
this kek
stinkers truly are failures in every conceivable way xD

62307471
>>62297950
oh look it's one of the fudcucks that admits to holding link (again). time to spam a screenshot of this OP in the next billion fud thread

item.title: DOT price targets — 4 replies — 5.0/hr

DOT price targets

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-03 04:30 UTC

62306358
the price target is zero.

Hell yeah

62307620

Not in a million years

like all alts, zero

item.title: WTF IS HAPPENING - preferably in haiku format — 8 replies — 3.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTF IS HAPPENING - preferably in haiku format

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-03 03:01 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62305942
>>62305920
You sold, did you not?
Is is a dead coin for Chuds.
I hope you all sold.

62305950
>>62305920
I just realized my dad's family was rich (and still rich), and I'm the only poorfag in his blood line...

62305965
>>62305920
Usual suspects
Cheating lying and thieving
Hold onto your butts

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62306038
>>62305920
Autumn leaves our shore.
Crypto winter is coming:
Bitcoin 10k soon.

62307302
>>62305920
CLARITY is close
A Hypeful summer is near
Tradfi buy in soon

62307326
MicroStrategy’s throne
He sells, the charts weep red tears
Hodlers curse his name

62307407
>>62305920
Tax season coming
Withdraw my staking rewards
Winter flowers bloom

62307430
>>62305920
>WTF IS HAPPENING - preferably in haiku format
Prices climb up high,Rates rise and the markets fall,Gold stalls, priced too high.

item.title: IT DOESN'T MATTER NOW WHAT HAPPENS, I WILL NEVER GIVE UP THE FIGHT THERE IS NO WAY I WILL RUN AWAY FROM ALL OF MY FRIGHT… — 3 replies — 0.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT DOESN'T MATTER NOW WHAT HAPPENS, I WILL NEVER GIVE UP THE FIGHT THERE IS NO WAY I WILL RUN AWAY FROM ALL OF MY FRIGHT…

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-06-03 00:26 UTC

OP

IT DOESN'T MATTER NOW WHAT HAPPENS, I WILL NEVER GIVE UP THE FIGHT
THERE IS NO WAY I WILL RUN AWAY FROM ALL OF MY FRIGHTS
LONG AS THE VOICE INSIDE ME SAYS "Go", I WILL ALWAYS KEEP ON LONGIN'
THERE IS NO WAY TO STOP ME FROM GOIN' TO THE VERY TOP
IT DOESN'T MATTER WHO IS WRONG OR WHO IS RIGHT

Early replies (first 3)

62306043
>THERE IS NO WAY TO STOP ME FROM GOIN' TO THE VERY TOP
nope
WEVE GOT TO MAKE IT REAL

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 92 replies — 17.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 92 • Updated: 2026-06-02 23:53 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62292012
what time of day do you make the trades

62292563
if you have a profitable strategy then just make a hyperliquid vault so we can join it and you have 10% profit share

62292730
>>62291779
There's a whole website and twitter account where you can check these things
>>62292012
>a broken clock predicts the future 80% of the time
That's one hell of a broken clock
>>62292012
The demo strategy executes ~2 hours into NY open, when there's enough FX data to confirm or inval the signal
>>62292563
Down the list of things to do. Right now it's a trade bot, which is being built because a mid-tier CEX wants to explore white labelling signals, so the bot needs to be optimized, but then I'll absolutely integrate it into HL

62292891
>>62292730
Thank you, I already have working strategies. I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.

62292918
>>62292891
>I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.
Always a good practice - prediction/outcome goes back to December 2024 which is where the hit rate is from. I could backtest to mid-2024 (when the FX layer was added) if I feel like it but I figured 100+ signals over more than a year was good enough

62293541
Spoonfeed me pls OP? Am I supposed to follow your twitter? How do I get the meme lines for myself.

62293559
>>62293541
>How do I get the meme lines for myself.
I'll post them in here, but there's also a live dashboard and a twitter, where a follow/likes would be greatly appreciated
>>62285878

62293571
>>62293559
Thanks for doing the needful sir. I'll be checking this out. Big ups anon.

62293619
>>62292730
Does the trading bot have contingencies in place just in case other trading bots start countertrading your strategy if it ends up being too successful?

62293680
>>62293619
I think if someone else's trading bot can move BTC by a few percent per day then we all have much bigger problems

62294595
>>62284814
Just buy the dip and sell the high simple. The main thing is to hold. I'm not only holding, I also staked some of my BTC recently since we can do that now without bridge involved.

62296711
BEHOLD, THE FUTURE

62296738
>>62296711

62297476
AAAAAAAAAAAAAA

62298184
>>62296711
Is that you Michael?

62298251
>>62284814
>buy signal
>5/28
This aged like milk.

62299491
>>62298251
>can't read
Maybe investing is not for you anon

>>62298184
Shhh it's a secret

62301262
>>62292563
>>62292730
what's a hyperliquid vault

62301440
>tfw if I listened to the schizo chart I would be down 10%

62301542
All i can see is that you've been incorrect? Am i missing something? Lol

62302104
>>62301542
>>62301440
We'll see - the hit rate's 78%, not 100%. The general sense, per the twitter thread yesterday, is bounce to the 5th-7th, then drop to the real bottom mid-June and then pump through summer, but obviously Brownian motion can't predict Saylor selling lol

62303698
Tell me no one bought because of this indicator please HAHAHAHAHA

62303805
Is this some intraday trading indicator? Like you were supposed to buy and then sell with leverage the day of the OP? If you would have bought and were holding now you would be fucked

62304332
reverse indicator of op is printing me money

62305278
Look how they massacred my coin

62305335
>>62296711
I appreciate the insight. This thread has been an interesting read.

62305363
>>62305335
Thanks Anon, wish it was going better right now though!
Liquidity's good, the sim says "increasing demand," sure enough stocks are up and copper/gold is acting like a boom is coming, but crypto is just getting slaughtered. The only explanation I can think of is we're getting fucked now that CME crypto futures are legal. But without any actual data that's probably just cope

62305385
it's over

62306031
>>62305363
I had an asset do 200% in like 7 days recently, so it seems at least that people aren't turning up their noses at it all, so long as substantial developments are being made. Still not immune to dipping after considering profit-taking. Good projects can stand out in a storm like a lighthouse does. We'll see if that continues into the year.

62306711
>>62306031
Oh, I don't think crypto's over at all, unless someone has cracked SHA-256. Something is definitely happening to the asset class, though. A pro-crypto Fed chair just took over, stocks are up, but our internet coins are acting like they're about to be banned (for the tenth time)

>>62307427

62307539

62303558

62304057
>>62303482
People are redeeming to buy into SpaceX's IPO.

62304069
EXECUTE ORDER 66

62304185
that was it, 67k was my cost basis for my bitcoin ETF. I capitulated. all crypto previously sold, all bitcoin etfs now sold. money moved into tech stocks

just holding 1 btc in cold storage now (which i will never sell)

now i can leave and never pay attention to this board again

62304200
>>62304185
Sell low, buy high, hell yeah

62304209
>>62304185

62304217

62304229

62304233
>>62304209

no thanks. if im wrong, i still have 1 btc

62304270
>>62304233

62304730

fucking lol retard good luck

62304748
>>62303482

>Too poor to gain anything from pumps
>Too poor to lose anything from dumps

62305479
>>62304069

WHY DID YOU SAY THAT FAGGOT

THIS IS UR FAULT

62305494
>>62304233

62305505
>>62304057
This - all capital is being sucked into AI stocks.

62305520
If we all close our eyes in exactly 5 minutes and repeat "the price is $100k" 10 times in a row, we can manifest it into reality.

Just try it. Please. I need this

62305628
>>62305520

62305631
>>62303482
hahaha smug faggots.

62305659
>>62304217

62305686

No one talks about crypto anymore unless they are talking about scams


No one invests in crypto anymore

62305724
>>62305686
>No one talks about crypto anymore unless they are talking about scams
Because that's all it is.

62305735
>>62305686

>>62305724
You are wrong.

62305739
It's unironically over. We're NGMI. Crypto is fucking dead, if you didn't already make it you never will now. Don't boughted, don't hodl. Just get what you can now before the entire crypto industry dies and you get nothing.

62305756
>>62305735
did you shart in your local mart today?

62305794
>>62305756

62305845
>>62303636
The best plan is to quit focusing on just transparent, centralized coins and move to private ones and (especially good) those that are only mineable via CPU and can't be pool mined, like Nerva. Those are going to be the future.

62305905
>>62305845

62305946
>>62305520

62305967

62305979

item.title: crypto shills destroyed /biz/ — 57 replies — 5.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
crypto shills destroyed /biz/

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-06-02 18:44 UTC

OP

you stupid shitcunts nobody is going to buy your fucken dumb digital coin that has no fucken use case

You were too late for bitcoin

you got psychologically destroyed by millennial and gen x goobers posting on twitter from their rented condos in miami

And now you're here trying to tiktok dance your way into fucken millions

GTFO

Early replies (first 3)

62293753
>>62293749
don't you have some dying boomer business to take care of lol

62293756
>>62293753
wowee

*does the fortnite dance*

feed my more low i.q. shortform slop sir!

Imagine being a GEN Z male who cannot even focus long enough to fill out a fucken business plan

62293763
>>62293756
imagine being so small brained that you have to work with dirty pipes since its the only way you wont lose everything. its indeed better you leave the crypto to smarter people and focus on the piss and shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62294231
>>62294221
wow, that was a very rude thing to say.

62294248
>>62293749
>crypto shills destroyed /biz/
/biz/ was created specifically to contain crypto shilling

62294250
>>62294194
>Chainlink solves multiple real world problems.
stopped reading right there, baggie

62294305
>>62294194
>Here's a really simple example for you: tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. This will enable the whole world to have exposure to hard money
Explain what this means. Seriously, explain it. Tokenized gold that's backed by physical gold. How is that any different than buying 'gold' on an exchange that's just a certificate? They're both digital, but one of them is just stored on a rube-goldberg mechanism instead of a simple database. How does it enable "the whole world" to have exposure to hard money?

You're just saying fucking words.

62294322
>>62294305
>how are tokenized assets different from legacy assets??

t.

62294326
>>62294322
Yes. In a qualitative way, not a quantitative way, explain how they are fundamentally different. Being stored on a slightly different digital substrate doesn't change : you hold a -promise- that you own gold, not actual physical gold.

62294328
>>62294326
Ask your local AI fuckhead.
Nobody here wants to explain the absolute basics of tokenization to you, even the banks have been trying to explain the benefits for over half a decade.

62294331
>>62294328
I want to see you explain it, because you can't, without regurgitating another explanation that will be fille with just as much of a smokescreen.

62294337
>>62294331
listen to yourself

62294340
>>62294337
>a really simple example for you
You're the one who said it. If it's so simple, surely you can bang out a coherent explanation in a minute or two.

You're a tech fetishist. You are mentally bound to this retarded world because you imagine it makes you smart, but it's really just smart in the "grandma thinks he's good with computer" kind of way.

62294365
>>62294340
Take a deep breath you fat boomer, your bp is spiking.

62294367
>>62293749
These sluts must know they are creating wankmaterial. They arent fooling anyone. Skanks. I hate it when they trick my brain into wanting to put my penis inside them. They would be raped 24/7 in the old days.

62294368
>>62294365
I accept your concession.

62294378
>>62293749
That's not even her real body. The app program she's using starts stuttering to keep up as she turns to the side. She's probably built like a soup can.

62294385
>>62293749
use cases are bearish desu

62294446
>>62294385
Probably why memes did so well last cycle. Why i am buying blue chip memes again while they are cooked.

62294649
>>62293756
can you clean my pipe?

62294739
>>62293749
I was at a Calgary Flames hockey game last year and there was a boomer ahead of me who must have drank 7 or 8 tallboys and was just watching shit like this on his phablet.

Sometimes you gotta respect the absolute zero regard boomers have.

62296803
>>62294194
nobody wants hard money lmfao
soft money is how the system works because they can just run w/e fucken scams they want
the system isn't for the people brother
gold was done away with for this exact reason

62298686
>>62293845
if you're so smart, why aren't you rich?
turns out you're fucking stupid.

62299806
>>62293749
just dont be too late for xrp

62301355
>>62293749
anyone who after years has not taken their involvement in crypto or more broadly business beyond shitposting about tokens on a uruguayan cattle milking forum is ngmi

62301664
I'm still bagholding shitcoins that I bought 4 years ago and I'm worse off now than when I first got into crypto. I feel nothing. No hope, no emotions. I don't know wtf to do anymore in life. I hardly have the energy to go to the cage. I just want my life to end

62301988
>>62301664
Take the loss, move the money into actual investments. An index fund, whatever. Watch it grow, because it is based on physical reality (the output of the entire country). Keep contributing to it. 10 years from now it will have compounded so much you'll forget this whole thing.

Literally, just be a normie. Normies are fucking rich these days as long as they invest carefully and don't just pour everything down doordash and rent.

62302350
>>62301988
>Yes goy, sell the crypto bottom and buy the S&P top

62302354
>>62293749
XMR has more of a use case than BTC at this point

62302369
>>62302350

62304020
>>62301988
>it is based on physical reality (the output of the entire country)
so it's the top then

62304948
>>62293749
Crypto shills are the entire reason for /biz/'s existence.

/biz/ was created as a containment board to get shitcoin shills off /g/.

Just like /mlp/ was created to get ponyfags off every other board.

62305222
>>62293749
This is literally our board, it was literally made for us originally. So YOU get out
